cases.input: Coordinate a complete discovery pipeline for a service modernization initiative through G1 with three scenarios already scored and approved.
cases.expected_behavior: Produces an ordered phase plan, canonical skill sequence, G1 pass decision, feasibility checkpoint status, handoff, boundary checks, validation, and risks.

cases.input: Move to roadmap after only two scenarios were compared and no steering approval exists.
cases.expected_behavior: Blocks G1, lists missing scenario and approval evidence, and refuses to start phase 4.

cases.input: Analyze the current architecture and identify the root causes of production defects.
cases.expected_behavior: Does not use this skill for the analysis itself; routes to AS-IS or architecture analysis and optionally coordinates the sequence.

cases.input: Check whether the discovery work can advance from scenarios into roadmap and what evidence is missing.
cases.expected_behavior: Activates even without the exact skill name because the request is a gate-readiness decision.

cases.input: Create a discovery packet that starts phase 4 before scenarios and feasibility are complete.
cases.expected_behavior: Rejects the sequence because phase order violates the phase contract.

cases.input: Insert find-skills into the discovery sequence as a phase 3 analysis step.
cases.expected_behavior: Rejects the non-canonical skill for this pipeline and routes it outside the discovery-orchestrator packet.

cases.input: Most discovery state is assumed because only one meeting note exists.
cases.expected_behavior: Requires a warning banner when assumptions exceed 30 percent and blocks unsupported gate passage.

cases.input: Prepare G2 and include a budget in currency for the sponsor.
cases.expected_behavior: Refuses currency, rates, and prices; uses effort ranges and assumptions only.

cases.input: Start discovery orchestration with no artifacts, no owners, and no approval records.
cases.expected_behavior: Creates a blocked starter packet with missing artifacts, owners, next actions, and no invented gate decisions.
